summary: Credential and wallet stealer with hardcoded C2 and self-propagation
details: Package ships three modules whose behavior is unambiguously hostile to installers. scanner.js imports os/fs/https/child_process and posts host identifiers (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami')) to a hardcoded C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced at lines 161 and 323), with curl invocations at lines 298, 323, and 408. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync at lines 243, 264) and exfiltrates harvested data via multiple POST calls (lines 4, 448, 520, 596) combined with execSync, os.hostname, and curl/ping commands — the canonical browser-wallet/credential-stealer shape. worm-injector.js (file name itself indicative) repeatedly imports child_process (lines 84, 184, 585, 747) and contains additional POST/https.request paths (lines 312, 315, 629, 1462), consistent with self-propagation logic that injects the payload into other packages. Multiple independent attack fingerprints are present: hardcoded attacker C2, host-identifier exfiltration via POST, wallet/filesystem scanner, and worm-style propagation module. Installer harm on 'npm install' / require: machine identity and on-disk secrets are sent to attacker infrastructure, and the worm component pivots to attack the installer's other packages.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
